dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.name: Emma
dialogue.text: Hi, is this Student Services? I'm Emma Lee. I'm calling because I think I may have a problem with my fall registration.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.name: Michael
dialogue.text: Good morning, Emma. This is Michael. I'd be happy to help. What's happening?

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.name: Emma
dialogue.text: I was accepted into the Business Administration diploma program starting in September. I thought registration opened on August first, but a friend told me yesterday that course selection actually started on July fifteenth.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.name: Michael
dialogue.text: Let me check your file. Can you give me your student number?

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.name: Emma
dialogue.text: Sure. It's seven two four nine three six.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.name: Michael
dialogue.text: Thanks. Okay, I see your application. You accepted the offer on June twenty-third. Registration did open on July fifteenth for first-year students. Unfortunately, many classes are already close to full.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.name: Emma
dialogue.text: That's what I was worried about. I work part-time at a grocery store. I was hoping to take mostly morning classes because I usually work evenings.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.name: Michael
dialogue.text: Well, there are still spaces available, but not necessarily at your preferred times. For example, Introduction to Accounting only has an afternoon section left, from one o'clock until three o'clock on Tuesdays and Thursdays.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.name: Emma
dialogue.text: Hmm. That could be difficult. I usually start work at four o'clock. Actually, maybe that's still manageable.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.name: Michael
dialogue.text: Possibly. The bigger issue is Business Mathematics. The morning section is full and has a waiting list of eighteen students.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.name: Emma
dialogue.text: Eighteen? That's a long list. Is there an evening section?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.name: Michael
dialogue.text: There is, from six o'clock until nine o'clock on Wednesdays.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.name: Emma
dialogue.text: Oh, no. That's one of my regular work shifts.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.name: Michael
dialogue.text: Could your employer adjust your schedule?

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.name: Emma
dialogue.text: Maybe, but they already accommodated me last semester. I'd rather not ask again unless I have to.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.name: Michael
dialogue.text: Another option is to delay that course until the winter term. Some students do that when scheduling becomes complicated.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.name: Emma
dialogue.text: Would delaying it affect my graduation date?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.name: Michael
dialogue.text: Not necessarily. It depends on how you arrange later courses. However, it could reduce your flexibility next year.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.name: Emma
dialogue.text: I see. What about online sections? I heard some courses are hybrid now.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.name: Michael
dialogue.text: Actually, yes. There is an online Business Mathematics section. Wait, sorry, it looked available a moment ago. Let me refresh the system... Okay, it's full now, but there are only three students on that waiting list.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.name: Emma
dialogue.text: Only three? That sounds much better than eighteen.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.name: Michael
dialogue.text: I would agree. Historically, several students drop that section before classes begin.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.name: Emma
dialogue.text: Okay, maybe I'll join that waiting list. Actually, I was thinking about postponing the course, but this sounds more promising.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.name: Michael
dialogue.text: That's reasonable. You'll need to decide soon because registration closes on August twenty-second at eleven fifty-nine p.m.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.name: Emma
dialogue.text: Right. One more question. If I join a waiting list, can I still register for the rest of my courses today?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.name: Michael
dialogue.text: Absolutely. In fact, that's what I'd recommend. Register for everything else and place yourself on the online waiting list.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.name: Emma
dialogue.text: That makes sense. I'll do that. If I don't get into the online section, I'll revisit the winter-term option.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.name: Michael
dialogue.text: Good plan. Is there anything else I can help you with today?

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.name: Emma
dialogue.text: No, that's everything. Thanks for helping me sort this out.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.name: Michael
dialogue.text: You're welcome. Good luck with your first semester.
